steps.title: Identify parallel and intersecting lines
steps.content: Since line $ r $ is parallel to line $ s $, they have the same slope. The angles formed by a transversal line with parallel lines are congruent or supplementary depending on their relative positions.

steps.title: Determine the relationship of angles
steps.content: From the attached figure, observe that line $ n $ intersects both $ r $ and $ s $. The vertical angle formed at the intersection with line $ r $ opposite to the given angle is equal to it. Thus, we equate this angle value to find $ x $.

steps.title: Calculate the measure of x
steps.content:
By observing the figure, we identify that angle opposite to $ 128^\circ $ is congruent due to parallel lines property. Therefore,
$ x + 128° = 180° $.
Solving for $ x $,
$$ x = 180° - 128° $$
B is correct.
